label: Attention Is All You Need
description: Vaswani et al. (2017) introduced the transformer architecture, replacing recurrence and convolution with multi-head self-attention. The foundational paper for modern LLMs.
abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

label: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3)
description: Brown et al. (2020) demonstrated that scaling language models to 175B parameters enables few-shot learning across a wide range of NLP tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.
abstract: We train GPT-3, an autoregressive language model with 175 billion parameters, and test its few-shot performance across NLP tasks. GPT-3 achieves strong performance on many benchmarks, often matching or exceeding the performance of fine-tuned models.

label: The Hallmarks of Aging
description: Lopez-Otin et al. (2013, updated 2023) defined the molecular and cellular hallmarks of aging, providing the foundational framework for longevity research.
abstract: Aging is characterized by a progressive loss of physiological integrity, leading to impaired function and increased vulnerability to death. We enumerateand describe the hallmarks of aging.

label: Why We Sleep (research basis)
description: Matthew Walker's research program on sleep and cognitive performance, establishing the mechanisms by which sleep deprivation impairs memory consolidation, emotional regulation, and immune function.
abstract: Sleep plays a critical role in cognitive performance, memory consolidation, and health. This body of work establishes the mechanisms and consequences of sleep insufficiency.

label: Fundamentals of Astrodynamics (Bate, Mueller, White)
description: The foundational textbook for orbital mechanics: two-body problem, orbital elements, maneuvers, and introductory mission design.
abstract: A classic introduction to astrodynamics covering the two-body problem, orbital elements, orbital maneuvers, and preliminary orbit determination.
